Ford.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford), [2](https://geraldrfordfoundation.org/uss-gerald-ford-cvn-78), [3](https://www.uscarriers.net/cvn78history.htm)

Gerald R. Ford served in the U.S. Naval Reserve during World War II, including combat duty aboard the light aircraft carrier USS Monterey in the Pacific Theater.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford), [3](https://www.uscarriers.net/cvn78history.htm), [4](https://www.airlant.usff.navy.mil/Organization/Aircraft-Carriers/USS-Gerald-R-Ford-CVN-78/Namesake-USS-Gerald-R-Ford/) He became Vice President in 1973 and then President in 1974 following the resignation of President Nixon.[4](https://www.airlant.usff.navy.mil/Organization/Aircraft-Carriers/USS-Gerald-R-Ford-CVN-78/Namesake-USS-Gerald-R-Ford/) The decision to name the aircraft carrier after him was announced in 2007 by Navy Secretary Donald Winter.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford), [5](https://geraldrfordfoundation.org/news-announcements/cvn-78-christening-uss-gerald-ford), [6](https://www.dvidshub.net/news/540449/navy-names-newest-carrier-after-president-ford)']}}

result:
The largest aircraft carrier in the world, the USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78), is named after the 38th President of the United States, Gerald R. Ford.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford), [2](https://geraldrfordfoundation.org/uss-gerald-ford-cvn-78), [3](https://www.uscarriers.net/cvn78history.htm)

Gerald R. Ford served in the U.S. Naval Reserve during World War II, including combat duty aboard the light aircraft carrier USS Monterey in the Pacific Theater.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford), [3](https://www.uscarriers.net/cvn78history.htm), [4](https://www.airlant.usff.navy.mil/Organization/Aircraft-Carriers/USS-Gerald-R-Ford-CVN-78/Namesake-USS-Gerald-R-Ford/) He became Vice President in 1973 and then President in 1974 following the resignation of President Nixon.[4](https://www.airlant.usff.navy.mil/Organization/Aircraft-Carriers/USS-Gerald-R-Ford-CVN-78/Namesake-USS-Gerald-R-Ford/) The decision to name the aircraft carrier after him was announced in 2007 by Navy Secretary Donald Winter.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford), [5](https://geraldrfordfoundation.org/news-announcements/cvn-78-christening-uss-gerald-ford), [6](https://www.dvidshub.net/news/540449/navy-names-newest-carrier-after-president-ford)
